symbol_prompt: A single vertical line intersected at the base by an open circle, with a second line branching upward and outward at a 45-degree angle from the vertical line.
lore: Grounding the standing leg engages the adductors to stabilize the pelvis. Counter-traction lengthens the spine, mitigating compression and encouraging axial extension.

symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, an off-center vertical line supports a smaller square resting on one corner. This second square is positioned to the right of the vertical line, suggesting grounded asymmetry and forward extension.
lore: The pose requires a precise adduction of the grounded leg while simultaneously lengthening the spine and extending forward, creating a stable, asymmetrical structure. The bound leg demands hip flexibility and controlled external rotation, contributing to pelvic stability.

symbol_prompt: An offset vertical axis represents the grounded, asymmetrical leg position. A solid bindu is placed at the base of the axis, symbolizing the focal point of grounding.

symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, two parallel vertical lines symbolize the extended legs. A solid bindu rests centrally between the lines, representing the grounded pelvis and core engagement.

symbol_prompt: The symbol consists of two parallel lines, representing balance and alignment, surmounted by an equilateral triangle, symbolizing the trinity of body, mind, and spirit in harmonious union within the pose.
lore: Ardha Matsyendrasana II requires the deep external rotation of one hip while maintaining the adduction of the opposite thigh. Focus on lifting the frontal iliac spines to lengthen the lumbar region while initiating a spinal twist from the thoracic vertebrae.

symbol_prompt: An offset vertical axis represents the grounded, asymmetrical leg position. A hexagonal vertex is placed at the base of the axis, symbolizing the tri-planar grounding and stability within the pose.
lore: The pose emphasizes adduction of the extended leg towards the midline and a corresponding axial extension of the spine. Focus is maintained on grounding through the supporting leg and ischial tuberosity.

iast_full: Vīrāsana Modified III (Parsva Virasana in Sirsasana)
symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, a central vertical hairline is intersected by two quarter-circle arcs. The arcs emanate from a point near the bottom of the frame, curving outwards and upwards to the left and right sides of the vertical line, representing the asymmetry of the legs.
lore: This variation inverts the foundational Sirsasana alignment with a lateral shift through Virasana leg positioning. Structural integrity derives from maintaining the vertical axis while introducing the asymmetrical leg position.

symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, two parallel vertical lines are positioned near the bottom, representing the grounding of the hands. Above these lines, two concentric arcs curve upwards and outwards from a central point between the lines, representing the lengthening spine and engagement of the core.
lore: This variation emphasizes shoulder extension while maintaining the core structural integrity of Adho Mukha Virasana. Precise adductor engagement and scapular stability are paramount for optimal spinal elongation.

symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, three intersecting lines form a central node. One line is vertical, bisecting the frame. The other two lines form an 'X' shape across the vertical line, originating close to the center and extending outwards towards the corners. An open circle is centered over the intersection, representing the balance and grounding.
lore: This variation emphasizes engagement of the quadriceps and hamstrings to stabilize the forward fold. Deepening the pose requires maintaining spinal extension and adductor engagement to draw the legs inward.

symbol_prompt: Two parallel horizontal lines represent the shoulder and hip girdles. A square frame encloses them, signifying the integrated structure of the pose.
lore: Astavakrasana requires adductor engagement to maintain leg position and spinal stability; the spine extends laterally while rotating, balancing on the hands.

symbol_prompt: A hexagon frame with a central vertical hairline extending from the top vertex to the bottom. Two shorter symmetrical hairlines extend outward from the central line at the midpoint, forming obtuse angles.
lore: Hamsasana requires precise alignment of the hands and shoulders, cultivating balance through core engagement and adductor stabilization. The posture strengthens the arms, wrists, and core while promoting spinal extension.

symbol_prompt: A hexagon frame contains three intersecting lines. One vertical line bisects the hexagon. Two lines intersect this vertical line above and below the center, forming two 'V' shapes that mirror each other. The vertices of the 'V' shapes reside on the central line.
lore: This pose initiates spinal flexion with deep adduction of the thighs, requiring significant flexibility to bind the hands behind the back, mirroring a tortoise withdrawing into its shell.

symbol_prompt: A square frame with a central vertical hairline. A single open circle sits above the frame, connected to the hairline. A horizontal hairline bisects the vertical line within the frame, positioned towards the bottom.
lore: Eka Pada Sirsasana requires deep external rotation of the hip, adduction to draw the foot to the head, and spinal elongation for balance. The structural challenge lies in maintaining a stable base while creating space for the leg.

symbol_prompt: A square frame contains three intersecting lines. A vertical line bisects the square, acting as the spine's central axis. Two lines intersect this vertical line within the lower half of the square, forming an inverted 'V' shape. An open circle resides above the square frame, centered on the vertical line, symbolizing cranial space.
lore: This asana emphasizes deep hamstring engagement and sacral flexion. The pose aims to integrate the front and back body lines while stabilizing the hip joint.

symbol_prompt: A square frame contains a diagonal slash that ascends from the bottom left to the top right. An open circle sits in the upper right corner of the square, touching both the frame and the slash.
lore: This posture demands deep hip flexion and external rotation, facilitated by adductor engagement. Spinal extension is paramount, achieved through a strong foundation and upward lift, grounding through the ischial tuberosities and coccyx, stabilizing the sacroiliac joint.

symbol_prompt: A square frame contains three intersecting lines. A vertical line bisects the square. Two diagonal lines intersect the vertical line within the square, forming an 'X' shape. A solid bindu sits at the intersection of the vertical and diagonal lines.
lore: Ruchikasana emphasizes adduction and internal rotation of the thighs while maintaining spinal extension. The posture integrates grounding through the legs with a lift through the torso.

symbol_prompt: An equilateral triangle rests within a square frame, apex pointing downwards. A single hairline extends vertically from the midpoint of the triangle's base, bisecting the square frame.
lore: Yoganidrasana emphasizes deep hip flexion, external rotation, and adduction. The pose requires significant spinal flexibility to maintain alignment and prevent strain.

symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, three lines intersect. Two diagonal lines converge at the frame's center, forming an 'X'. An equilateral triangle sits above the 'X', its base aligned with the frame's top edge, apex pointing down.
lore: This pose necessitates significant adductor engagement for inner thigh compression and a rounded thoracic spine to counterbalance the extended legs.

symbol_prompt: A square frame contains a diagonal slash that ascends from the bottom left to the top right. An equilateral triangle rests atop the frame, its base coinciding with the frame's top edge, apex pointing down.
lore: The pose demands intense adductor engagement to lift, with a spiraling action along the spinal axis to maintain balance. Scapular protraction and upper back rounding create space for the arm bind.

symbol_prompt: A square frame contains a single horizontal line at the bottom, representing the ground. A bent line initiates from the midpoint of the horizontal line extending upwards and diagonally, ending near the top right corner, mirroring the angled body.
lore: This asana demands precise alignment of the humerus within the glenoid fossa while maintaining spinal extension. The adductors draw the femur medially, establishing a stable fulcrum for the levering action.

symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, three intersecting lines create a minimalist crane form. Two lines bisect the square diagonally from top corners to the bottom, forming a 'V' shape. A short horizontal line rests atop the 'V' at the vertical midpoint, suggesting the balancing leg.
lore: Compress the elbows towards the midline, engaging adductors. Elongate the spine, distributing the weight evenly through the hands.

symbol_prompt: The symbol consists of two parallel lines representing the grounded legs, and concentric arcs above them to signify the expanding energy of the torso and breath.
lore: Ashwa Sanchalanasana, a variation of the root pose, involves deep flexion of the hip joint in the forward leg, coupled with extension in the trailing leg; this requires the psoas major of the back leg to lengthen as the quadriceps of the front leg engage to stabilize the knee. Simultaneous engagement of the trapezius and serratus anterior facilitates opening the chest and lifting the frontal iliac spines, creating space in the abdomen.

symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, two opposing diagonal slashes originate from the top corners, descending and converging towards the center of the bottom edge. A horizontal line bisects the frame, reinforcing the grounding action.
lore: This pose emphasizes adduction of the scapulae and expansion of the pectoral girdle while grounding through the feet. The spine remains neutral, resisting extension.

iast_full: Dwi Pāda Viparīta Dạṇdāsana Modified II (On a chair)
symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, a chair is represented by a horizontal line. A hairline, representing the spine, leans laterally from the midpoint. A solid bindu marks the grounding sit bone on the chair.
lore: Chair modification emphasizes controlled adduction, countering abduction. Stabilized sacrum allows for lengthened spine, promoting space for lateral extension along torso.

iast_full: Dwi Pāda Viparīta Dạṇdāsana Modified I (Cross bolsters)
symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, a vertical hairline is offset to the left. A small solid square is positioned on the right edge, slightly above the frame's center, with an angled line extending from its lower-left corner towards the vertical hairline, signifying the asymmetrical support and directional force.
lore: This variation emphasizes spinal rotation and lateral flexion with support. Bolsters facilitate deeper access by providing a stable base and promoting adductor release, enhancing the spinal twist.

iast_full: Baddha Kọnāsana Modified I (Supta Baddha Konasana)
symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, two parallel vertical lines are positioned slightly apart. A hexagonal vertex, oriented with two points on the horizontal and four points on the vertical, is centered between the lines, representing the bound angle and symmetrical alignment.
lore: Supta Baddha Konasana I encourages a release in the hip flexors and groin. The adductors draw towards the midline facilitating sacral alignment and grounding through the back body.

symbol_prompt: Three intersecting lines form a central triangle. A solid bindu is placed at the intersection point.
lore: Bhekasana emphasizes deep quadriceps engagement and adduction to externally rotate the hips while maintaining spinal extension. The pose necessitates both flexibility and controlled muscular engagement for proper alignment.

symbol_prompt: A diagonal slash ascends from left to right. A solid bindu is placed at the base of the slash, representing the root.
lore: This variation emphasizes adductor engagement and maintaining verticality through the torso while the hips are in adduction and external rotation. The spine is kept elongated, counteracting compression from the seated position.

symbol_prompt: An equilateral triangle rests on its apex. A single vertical hairline bisects the triangle, extending slightly beyond its base.
lore: Arms are levers balancing torso; adductors compress around the humerus. Spine remains neutral, relying on core stability against gravity's load.

symbol_prompt: An equilateral triangle rests on its apex. Three lines bisect the triangle: one vertical, one angled right, and one angled left, all intersecting at the triangle's centroid.
lore: Heels pressed into the perineum create a stable base. Opposing forces of grounding and spinal extension lengthen the core, promoting stability and awareness.

symbol_prompt: An offset vertical axis represents the grounded, asymmetrical leg position. An equilateral triangle rests on the vertical line above the offset point, apex down, symbolizing the contained core and lifted torso.
lore: Baddha Padmasana demands intense adduction of the thighs and complete external rotation of the hips.  Simultaneously, it requires significant spine elongation through the crown of the head.

symbol_prompt: Three intersecting lines form a central triangle. Concentric arcs emanate from the triangle's base, suggesting rotation.
lore: The pose integrates axial rotation with the inverted foundation of *Sirsasana*. Primary structure derives from adductor engagement of the grounded leg and oblique activation for spinal twist.

symbol_prompt: An offset vertical axis represents the grounded, asymmetrical leg position. Concentric arcs emanate from the offset point, extending outwards and suggesting a spiraling rotation around the central axis.
lore: This pose cultivates spinal length through axial rotation anchored by the seated bones. The engaged adductors of the straight leg provide stability and facilitate deeper spinal articulation, promoting core integration.

symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, a vertical hairline ascends from the bottom edge. Three concentric semi-circular arcs, their open ends facing upwards, are stacked above it, representing the progressive lift of the pelvic floor.
lore: The pose utilizes the wall as a primary structural support, creating a right angle at the hip crease. Consistent adductor engagement initiates the return of venous blood flow.

symbol_prompt: A square frame contains three intersecting lines. One line runs vertically, bisecting the square. The other two lines originate from the bottom corners of the square, intersecting the vertical line at a point above the square's center, forming an inverted triangular shape within the square.
lore: This pose requires a lifting action of the pelvis while maintaining spinal extension. The adductors draw the legs inward to stabilize the base.

symbol_prompt: A square frame contains a diagonal slash that ascends from the bottom right to the top left. A small circle is tangent to the slash, positioned just below the frame's top edge, symbolizing the point of balance.
lore: Yogadandasana is characterized by intense hamstring engagement and hip flexion. The primary structural element is the consolidation of core strength to maintain equilibrium while balancing on a single supporting leg and arm.

symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, a single angled line ascends from the bottom left to the top right, representing the spine. A small open circle rests on the line, slightly off-center, symbolizing the single point of balance.
lore: The pose demands maximal spinal extension and engagement of the quadriceps of the weight-bearing leg for stability. Adductor engagement of the lifted leg promotes internal rotation and hip alignment.

symbol_prompt: A diagonal slash bisected by an open circle represents the pose, signifying the grounded yet expansive energy of the warrior.
lore: In Virabhadrasana II, the front thigh externally rotates while the back thigh internally rotates to stabilize the pelvis; simultaneously, the frontal iliac spines lift, engaging the core and lengthening the lumbar spine.

symbol_prompt: The symbol consists of three intersecting lines forming an equilateral triangle at their intersection. This represents the balance of three energies, or the sthira and sukham aspects of the pose.
lore: In Garudasana, the femurs adduct and internally rotate while the scapulae abduct. The deep core muscles stabilize the spine as the frontal iliac spines lift, promoting a sense of groundedness and balance.

iast_full: Ūrdhva Prasārita Pādāsana Modified I (Near a wall)
symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, a central vertical hairline extends from the top to bottom edge. An equilateral triangle, pointing upwards, intersects the vertical line at its midpoint. The triangle's base is parallel to the bottom edge of the frame.
lore: This variation enhances proprioceptive awareness through wall contact. The structural focus is on maintaining a neutral spine while deepening the hip flexion.

symbol_prompt: A single horizontal line intersected by a smaller, perpendicular line at one end, within a hexagon. The horizontal represents the grounded body, the perpendicular the lifted leg, and the hexagon the core integration.
lore: Anantasana focuses on maintaining a lateral spinal extension while engaging the adductors of the lifted leg. The grounding leg provides a stable base, allowing for controlled rotation and lengthening.

symbol_prompt: A square frame contains a diagonal slash that ascends from the bottom left to the top right, representing spinal extension. An open circle intersects the slash, offset towards the upper right corner of the frame, symbolizing the single supporting leg and its balancing act.
lore: This pose integrates single leg balance with a deep backbend, demanding precise spinal extension and engagement of the standing leg's adductors to maintain stability. The lifted leg increases the pose's difficulty, testing core strength and hamstring flexibility.

symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, a diagonal slash ascends from the bottom left to the top right, representing spinal extension. A solid bindu sits below the line, offset towards the left corner of the frame, symbolizing the grounding action of the supporting leg.
lore: Kapotasana requires deep spinal extension and opening of the chest and hip flexors. The pose relies on engaging the back muscles while maintaining a stable base through the thighs and pelvic floor.

symbol_prompt: Two parallel lines represent the extended and flexed legs. A hexagon bisects the lines creating a vertex representing the aligned torso.
lore: Posterior leg strongly grounded with adductor engagement. Anterior knee aligned directly over the ankle creating 90 degree angle. Spine maintains verticality, opposing forward movement of the front knee.

symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, an equilateral triangle rests on its apex at the bottom center. A single hairline extends vertically from the triangle's peak, bisecting it and reaching the top of the frame, representing the spinal axis.
lore: Valakhilyasana emphasizes adductor engagement to maintain medial stability. The spine must remain elongated and neutral to support the leveraged balance.

symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, three lines intersect, forming an inverted 'Y'. The central vertical line represents spinal alignment. The two angled lines, bisecting the lower quadrants, symbolize the grounding leg and lifted leg. An equilateral triangle is positioned at the apex of the 'Y', pointing downwards, representing the sacrum.
lore: This advanced variation focuses on deep hip opening and spinal extension. The adductor magnus draws the femur inward, while latissimus dorsi assists in spinal arch.

symbol_prompt: A square frame contains a diagonal slash that ascends from the bottom left to the top right, representing spinal extension. An equilateral triangle rests on the slash, offset towards the bottom left corner of the frame, symbolizing the single grounded hip and its foundational stability.
lore: Bhujangasana II emphasizes lumbar extension and spinal articulation. The supporting leg facilitates hip adduction and internal rotation, establishing a stable base.

symbol_prompt: Three intersecting lines form a central hexagonal vertex. The lines represent the core, legs, and spine, converging in balanced opposition.
lore: The pose requires sustained adductor engagement for leg integrity and reciprocal spinal extension to counteract pelvic flexion. The posterior pelvic tilt serves as the foundation for hamstring origin anchoring.

symbol_prompt: An offset vertical axis represents the grounded, asymmetrical leg position. A hexagonal vertex sits adjacent to the vertical axis, symbolizing core integration and balanced energy flow in the posture.
lore: Buddhasana facilitates deep hip opening and grounds the ischial tuberosities, providing a stable base. The pose requires adductor engagement for leg stability and encourages spinal elongation through the crown of the head.

symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, three lines intersect at a central bindu. A vertical line bisects the square; two concentric arcs, one above the other, are positioned on either side of the vertical line, representing the hip and chest relationship.
lore: This posture establishes fundamental adductor engagement and spinal elongation. The grounded leg forms the primary foundation, from which the angled leg initiates a spiral action through the torso.

symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, a vertical hairline is offset to the left, representing the grounded leg. Two concentric arcs, emanating from the right side of the frame, symbolize the angled torso and open hip of the extended leg.
lore: This asana emphasizes a strong foundation through the grounded leg and hip, while the opposing leg extends, fostering hip opening and spinal extension. The adductor muscles play a crucial role in maintaining inner thigh engagement and stability.

symbol_prompt: A diagonal slash descends from left to right, bisecting a hexagonal vertex at its midpoint. The hexagon represents the integrated core; the slash the flexed spine.
lore: Kurmasana embodies a complete spinal flexion while simultaneously externally rotating the femurs. Deep engagement of the adductors stabilizes the pose and promotes spinal extension against the flexion.

symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, two parallel vertical lines represent the legs providing foundational support. A square rests atop the lines. This square form symbolizes the head.
lore: This posture's structure relies on strong adductor engagement of the thighs while maintaining a precise spinal extension from the sacrum through the cervical vertebrae. Scapular stabilization is critical for supporting the pose.

symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, a diagonal slash ascends from the bottom left to the top right, symbolizing spinal extension. A small, filled square is positioned along the diagonal, offset towards the upper right corner of the frame, representing the lifted leg and its counterbalancing action.
lore: Natarajasana requires engagement of the standing leg adductors to maintain midline stability. Spinal extension and controlled backbend originate from a stabilized sacrum.

symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, a vertical hairline is centrally placed. A filled circle resides at the base of the line, symbolizing the engaged navel point. Above the circle, a concave arc intersects the vertical line, depicting the lifted diaphragm.
lore: Uddiyana Bandha involves the forceful lifting of the diaphragm and abdominal organs towards the spine, creating a vacuum within the thoracic cavity. This action requires precise engagement of the abdominal muscles and stabilization of the pelvic floor to maintain the structural integrity of the pose.

symbol_prompt: A vertical hairline, slightly offset from the center, represents the spinal axis.  An open circle is positioned along the line, indicating the point of maximum spinal twist.
lore: This asana's structural blueprint involves a twisting action around the spine, achieved through adductor engagement and controlled spinal elongation. Grounding through the sit bones anchors the pose.

symbol_prompt: A series of horizontal parallel lines intersected by a single vertical axis, representing the expansion of the lungs.
lore: The science of breath control. Focus on the expansion of the thoracic cage and the steady, rhythmic flow of Prana through the Nadis.

iast_full: Mālāsana I Modified I (Near a wall)
symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, two parallel horizontal lines represent the wall and grounded foot. Above these lines, a single vertical line ascends, representing the spine's verticality and extension assisted by the wall.
lore: Wall provides support, enabling precise axial extension. Action involves engagement of adductors and quadriceps for stability, with conscious elongation of the spine.

symbol_prompt: A single hairline traces concentric arcs, symbolizing the pose's complex interplay of opposing forces and the grounded yet expansive nature of advanced asana practice.
lore: In Dwi Pada Koundinyasana, the deep engagement of Serratus Anterior protracts the scapulae, stabilizing the shoulder joint as the humerus internally rotates to support the legs; simultaneously, rectus abdominis and psoas major work eccentrically to control spinal extension while lifting the frontal iliac spines to maintain balance.

symbol_prompt: The symbol features a diagonal slash representing the body's angled position, intersected by concentric arcs symbolizing the engaged core muscles spiraling around the spine.
lore: In Ubhaya Padangusthasana, the psoas major and iliacus muscles engage to flex the hip joints while the quadriceps extend the knees. Simultaneously, the deep core muscles stabilize the spine as the hamstrings lengthen, allowing for a balanced, seated posture.

symbol_prompt: A series of horizontal parallel lines intersected by a single vertical axis, representing the expansion of the lungs.
lore: The science of breath control. Focus on the expansion of the thoracic cage and the steady, rhythmic flow of Prana through the Nadis.

symbol_prompt: The symbol for Pasasana features a stylized human figure within a square frame, illustrating the deep squat and spinal twist characteristic of the pose; the figure is slightly offset from the vertical axis of the square to emphasize the asymmetrical nature of the bind and the spinal rotation involved.
lore: Pasasana requires deep flexion of the knees and hips, demanding considerable ankle dorsiflexion and external rotation of the thighs; simultaneously, the pose necessitates spinal extension achieved by engaging the erector spinae and lifting the frontal iliac spines to prevent excessive rounding of the back. Furthermore, the binding action promotes scapular protraction and facilitates a stretch across the upper back muscles, while requiring significant shoulder internal rotation to clasp the hands behind.

symbol_prompt: An offset vertical axis represents the asymmetrical spinal position. An open circle sits along the line, away from the center, signifying the abdominal twist.
lore: This pose emphasizes spinal rotation while maintaining a grounded base, lengthening the spine from the sacrum to the crown of the head. Adductor engagement stabilizes the legs, resisting the rotational force.

symbol_prompt: A minimalist icon with a single hairline within a square frame, representing the subtle yet powerful engagement of the root lock.
lore: Mulabhandasana, a variation of root pose, involves contracting the pelvic floor muscles while grounding the ischial tuberosities and lifting the frontal iliac spines to engage the core. Simultaneously, the pose facilitates internal rotation of the thighs, stabilizing the hip joint and lengthening the sacrum away from the lumbar vertebrae.

symbol_prompt: A diagonal slash cutting across a square frame symbolizes the dynamic opposition of forces inherent in Visvamitrasana. The slash represents the linear extension and stretch, while the square frame signifies the grounded stability and containment required to execute the pose safely.
lore: Visvamitrasana demands significant hamstring flexibility in the extended leg to facilitate external rotation at the hip, coupled with robust shoulder girdle abduction and scapular protraction to maintain stability in the supporting arm. Activation of the transverse abdominis and multifidus muscles is crucial for spinal stabilization and preventing excessive lumbar extension throughout the asana.

symbol_prompt: A diagonal slash bisects an open circle. The slash represents the dynamic line of force from the feet to the spine; the circle embodies the integrated core.
lore: Urdhva Dandasana establishes the foundation for advanced backbends by emphasizing quadriceps engagement and spinal extension. The posture builds balanced strength through reciprocal flexion and extension vectors.

symbol_prompt: The symbol features three intersecting lines, creating a central triangular space, with vertices at the intersection points forming hexagonal patterns, symbolizing balance and foundation.
lore: In Halasana, the erector spinae powerfully lengthens to facilitate spinal flexion while the hamstrings undergo an intense stretch, requiring deep engagement of the quadriceps to protect the knees. Scapular adduction and depression support the pose, facilitating a broadening of the upper back and optimal spinal articulation.

symbol_prompt: A central vertical hairline represents the spine. A single, shorter horizontal hairline extends from the bottom of the vertical line, angled slightly to suggest asymmetry.  A solid bindu is placed at the top of the vertical line, above the intersection.
lore: Eka Pada Sarvangasana requires meticulous adductor engagement in the extended leg while maintaining spinal alignment in the inverted torso.  The pose builds toward deeper backbends and abdominal control through unilateral challenge.

symbol_prompt: A thin, single hairline forms a circle, encapsulating a solid, centrally positioned bindu.
lore: Laghuvajrasana initiates with deep spinal flexion, counteracted by eccentric contraction of the quadriceps to control knee flexion; simultaneously, there's an engagement of the erector spinae to prevent complete rounding of the thoracic spine, while stabilizing the pose.

symbol_prompt: The symbol consists of three intersecting lines representing the grounding, balance, and extension required in the pose, while the open circle symbolizes the opening of the hips and the energetic flow achieved through dedicated practice.
lore: Galavasana requires intense hip flexion and external rotation in the lifted leg, engaging the gluteus medius and piriformis. Simultaneously, the standing leg demands quadriceps activation and ankle stabilization, preventing medial collapse through engagement of the tibialis anterior.

symbol_prompt: A single hairline bisects an open circle, signifying the balance between focused extension and the open awareness required in Trikonasana.
lore: In Uttihita Trikonasana, the standing leg femur internally rotates while the opposite side hip abducts, facilitating the extension of the spine; simultaneously, Serratus Anterior draws the scapula laterally around the ribcage, enhancing the reach of the top arm.

symbol_prompt: The symbol consists of two parallel lines representing the grounding legs, with a solid bindu (dot) above, symbolizing the focused energy and mental stillness achieved in the pose.
lore: Padahasthasana involves a deep forward bend, promoting lengthening of the hamstrings and spine. The pose encourages engagement of the abdominal muscles and a release of tension in the neck as the head hangs freely.

symbol_prompt: The symbol features an offset vertical axis crossed by concentric arcs that suggest the opening of the hips and engagement of the core in the pose.
lore: In Supta Vajrasana, the quadriceps lengthen intensely as the knees flex towards the floor, while the abdominal muscles engage to lift the frontal iliac spines towards the sternum, preserving lumbar curve. The psoas muscles lengthen eccentrically to allow the thighs to open in external rotation.

symbol_prompt: A series of horizontal parallel lines intersected by a single vertical axis, representing the expansion of the lungs.
lore: The science of breath control. Focus on the expansion of the thoracic cage and the steady, rhythmic flow of Prana through the Nadis.

symbol_prompt: Three lines intersect at a central point. A solid bindu is placed at the end of the longest line, away from the intersection.
lore: The adductors of the grounded leg strongly engage to stabilize the base. Axial extension of the spine is paramount, preventing lateral collapse.

symbol_prompt: The symbol features an open circle with a vertical axis offset to one side, representing the lifting action and spinal extension in Setu Bandhasana.
lore: In Setu Bandhasana, the gluteus maximus and hamstrings contract to lift the pelvis, promoting extension of the lumbar spine while the sternum moves towards the chin; the internal rotation of the thighs helps stabilize the pose and lift the frontal iliac spines.

symbol_prompt: A bow with an arrow pulled back to the ear. The bow is represented by a thin, elegant line, and a solid bindu marks the point of focus.
lore: Akarna Dhanurasana powerfully stretches the hamstrings and hip abductors of one leg while simultaneously engaging the hip flexors of the opposite leg; the pose requires deep external rotation of the hip of the 'bowstring' leg and the active lifting of the frontal iliac spines to maintain the integrity of the lumbar curve.

symbol_prompt: The symbol features three intersecting lines representing the spine and the two legs converging towards the center, symbolizing the core engagement and grounding aspect of the pose; the solid bindu represents the drishti, or focal point, crucial for maintaining balance and concentration.
lore: Salabhasana initiates with the deep spinal extensors, like the multifidus, engaging to lift the thorax, while the gluteus maximus facilitates hip extension and the internal rotation of the thighs; simultaneously, the pose requires lifting the frontal iliac spines away from the floor to intensify the backbend and engage the core. Precise alignment involves lengthening the sacrum towards the heels and actively drawing the shoulder blades down the back, thus opening the chest and fostering a powerful yet sustainable spinal extension.

symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, a vertical hairline bisects the frame. Two additional hairlines originate from the bottom corners of the frame, angled inwards and upwards to intersect the vertical line at a point slightly below the frame's center, creating an inverted 'V' shape, symbolizing transference of weight and base stability.
lore: This variation integrates the foundational stability of Sirsasana with the arm balance dynamics of Bakasana. Key blueprint involves maintaining a stable shoulder girdle while engaging the core to lift and transfer weight onto the arms.

symbol_prompt: The symbol merges a diagonal slash (/) representing the asymmetry of the pose, suggesting imbalance and challenge, with a solid bindu (•) to denote grounding and focused awareness.  The slash symbolizes the dynamic, twisting action, while the bindu embodies the concentrated energy required for stability.
lore: Vatayanasana requires a deep external rotation of the hip joint in the flexed leg, engaging the gluteus medius and piriformis, while simultaneously lifting the frontal iliac spines to maintain an upright torso. The standing leg demands significant quadriceps activation and stabilization through the ankle joint to support the asymmetrical weight distribution.

iast_full: Supta Vīrāsana Modified I (On a bolster)
symbol_prompt: Building upon the Virasana base, the two parallel vertical lines remain, but they are capped at the top by a wide, gently arced parabola facing upwards. This combination symbolizes the anchored, stable grounding of the shins and feet contrasted with the expansive, reclining opening of the chest and heart center.
lore: Virasana aligns the spine vertically by grounding the ischial tuberosities evenly between the heels, promoting a neutral pelvic tilt. Precise adductor engagement draws the inner knees toward the midline for stability.

symbol_prompt: The symbol consists of three intersecting lines symbolizing the grounding and centering aspects of the pose, intersected by concentric arcs which represent the blooming lotus and the expansive energy cultivated in Padmasana.
lore: Padmasana requires deep external rotation of the hips, facilitated by the piriformis and obturator muscles, allowing the feet to rest comfortably on the opposite thighs. The pose necessitates lifting the frontal iliac spines to maintain an upright spinal alignment and prevent lumbar compression.

symbol_prompt: A single hairline forming a stylized fetus within a hexagon, symbolizing introspection and the fetal position.
lore: In Pindasana, the spine deeply flexes, drawing the knees towards the face while maintaining the shoulder stand foundation; this involves maximal contraction of abdominal muscles and a rounding of the thoracic and lumbar vertebrae.

iast_full: Ūrdhva Dhanurāsana I Modified I (from Sirsasana)
symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, two parallel vertical lines are positioned centrally, signifying balanced weight distribution on the forearms. A single horizontal line bisects the square, intersecting the vertical lines at their midpoints, creating a 'T' shape at each point, representing stabilized inversion.
lore: The pose requires precise engagement of the shoulder girdle and trapezius muscles for stability. Axial extension minimizes spinal compression, creating space between vertebrae.

lore: The science of breath control. Focus on the expansion of the thoracic cage and the steady, rhythmic flow of Prana through the Nadis.

symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, a diagonal slash ascends from the bottom left to the top right, representing the flow of energy. A solid bindu resides near the midpoint of the slash, weighted slightly toward the bottom of the frame, signifying grounded awareness.
lore: The pose establishes the foundation for standing postures, emphasizing adductor engagement and sacral alignment. Proper execution requires conscious effort to maintain a neutral spine and evenly distribute weight.

symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, a diagonal slash ascends from the bottom left. An equilateral triangle, its apex pointing downwards, is positioned directly above the slash, bisected by the top right corner of the frame.
lore: Dhyana necessitates spinal extension, achieved via abdominal engagement and adductor contraction; foundationally, it cultivates the energetic 'mulabandha'.

symbol_prompt: The symbol features a vertical axis slightly offset to the side, representing the subtle asymmetry of the pose and breath. A solid bindu is placed at the center, symbolizing the grounding and centering effect of muladhara bandha.
lore: In this variation, the muladhara bandha engages the pelvic floor, drawing the perineum inward and upward. Simultaneously, the frontal iliac spines lift as the sacrum broadens, stabilizing the lumbar spine.

symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, two parallel vertical lines represent the grounding legs. Three concentric semi-circular arcs, their open ends facing upwards and bisected by the lines, suggest the controlled contraction and energy containment.
lore: Mula Bandha involves the contraction of the pelvic floor muscles, specifically the perineum. This action draws energy upwards, stabilizing the base of the spine and activating core support.

symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, a vertical hairline is offset to the left. A solid bindu sits on the right, bisected by the horizontal midline of the square, representing the grounded yet asymmetrical nature of the pose.
lore: Sanchalana activates the adductors to stabilize the pose, facilitating spinal extension through the engaged core.

symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, a diagonal slash ascends from the bottom left to the top right, representing dynamic suspension. Two concentric arcs, their open ends facing upwards, are positioned below the slash, suggesting grounded stability.
lore: Jhulana’s blueprint emphasizes the engagement of core stabilizers to maintain equilibrium while the body suspends. Precise adductor engagement facilitates balanced suspension.

symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, two parallel horizontal lines represent the pectoral girdle. A downward-pointing isosceles triangle rests centrally beneath the lines, its apex near the bottom of the frame, symbolizing the grounding sternum.
lore: Simultaneous placement of eight points on the ground creates a stable structural base. Adductor engagement and spinal extension counteract the force of gravity.

symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, a vertical hairline is offset to the right. A crescent shape, its curve facing left, is positioned on the left side of the frame, suggesting the half-opening and the grounded hip.
lore: Ardha Padmasana initiates hip opening and spinal alignment. The pose structurally facilitates internal rotation of one hip and external rotation of the other while encouraging vertical lift through the torso.

symbol_prompt: The symbol consists of a diagonal slash bisected by a solid bindu.
lore: In Swastikasana, the femur heads deeply externally rotate while the sacrum gently releases towards the floor. Precise engagement of the pelvic floor muscles supports the lifting of the frontal iliac spines, promoting an upright spinal column.

symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, three intersecting lines form a central equilateral triangle. The triangle's apex points upwards, while the lines extend beyond the triangle's vertices, bisecting the frame's sides, representing the distribution of axial extension and energetic balance.
lore: This variation emphasizes hamstring release while maintaining spinal integrity. The adductors draw inward to support the lift, while the spine elongates from the sacrum to the crown of the head.

symbol_prompt: Within a square frame, a diagonal slash descends from the top right to the bottom left. An equilateral triangle is positioned such that its base aligns with the bottom of the frame, and its apex intersects the diagonal slash near the frame's vertical center, symbolizing supported inversion and foundational alignment.
lore: This variation utilizes a bolster to facilitate spinal extension and anterior hip opening. It supports a greater range of adductor engagement, promoting stability throughout the pose.

symbol_prompt: A central vertical line signifies spinal extension. Two intersecting lines angled outwards from the base of the vertical line depict adducted legs. Concentric arcs emanate from the central point, representing expansive energy.
lore: This variation emphasizes adduction, drawing the legs toward the midline while maintaining spinal extension. Grounding through the feet stabilizes the pose as the torso lengthens.
